volumeTitle: Trojan Women. Iphigenia among the Taurians. Ion
label: Ion (Section 526 - Section 557b)

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: οὐ φιλῶ φρενοῦν ἀμούσους καὶ μεμηνότας ξένους .
sections.translation: I am not fond of schooling boors and crazy strangers .

sections.label: Section 527
sections.original: κτεῖνε καὶ πίμπρη · πατρὸς γάρ , ἢν κτάνῃς , ἔσῃ φονεύς .
sections.translation: Kill me , burn me , if thou wilt ; for , if thou dost , thou wilt be thy father ’ s murderer .

sections.label: Section 528
sections.original: ποῦ δέ μοι πατὴρ σύ ; ταῦτʼ οὖν οὐ γέλως κλύειν ἐμοῦ ;
sections.translation: Thou my father , indeed i Oh ! is not news like this enough to make me laugh ?

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: οὔ · τρέχων ὁ μῦθος ἄν σοι τἀμὰ σημήνειεν ἄν .
sections.translation: Not so ; my tale , as it proceeds , will prove to thee what I assert .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.translation: Pray , what hast thou to tell me ?

sections.label: Section 530b
sections.original: πατὴρ σός εἰμι καὶ σὺ παῖς ἐμός .
sections.translation: That I am thy own father , and thou my very child .

sections.label: Section 531
sections.translation: Who says so ?

sections.label: Section 531b
sections.original: ὅς σʼ ἔθρεψεν ὄντα Λοξίας ἐμόν .
sections.translation: Loxias , who gave thee nurture , though thou wert my son .

sections.label: Section 532
sections.translation: Thou art thy own witness .

sections.label: Section 532b
sections.original: τὰ τοῦ θεοῦ γʼ ἐκμαθὼν χρηστήρια .
sections.translation: Nay , I have learnt the answer of the god .

sections.label: Section 533
sections.translation: Thou art mistaken in the dark riddle thou hast heard .

sections.label: Section 533b
sections.translation: It seems then I do not hear aright .

sections.label: Section 534
sections.original: ὁ δὲ λόγος τίς ἐστι Φοίβου ;
sections.translation: What said Phoebus ?

sections.label: Section 534b
sections.translation: That the man who met me —

sections.label: Section 535
sections.translation: When and where ?

sections.label: Section 535b
sections.translation: As I came forth from the god ’ s temple —

sections.label: Section 536
sections.translation: Well ! what should happen to him ?

sections.label: Section 536b
sections.translation: Should be my own true son .

sections.label: Section 537
sections.translation: Thy own true son , or a gift from others ?

sections.label: Section 537b
sections.translation: A gift , but mine for all that .

sections.label: Section 538
sections.original: πρῶτα δῆτʼ ἐμοὶ ξυνάπτεις πόδα σόν ;
sections.translation: Am I the first that thou didst meet ?

sections.label: Section 538b
sections.translation: I have met no other , my son .

sections.label: Section 539
sections.translation: Whence came this piece of luck ?

sections.label: Section 539b
sections.translation: To both of us alike it causes surprise .

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: ἔα . τίνος δέ σοι πέφυκα μητρός ;
sections.translation: Ah I but who was my mother ?

sections.label: Section 540b
sections.translation: I cannot tell .

sections.label: Section 541
sections.translation: Did not Phoebus tell thee that ?

sections.label: Section 541b
sections.translation: I was so pleased with this , I did not ask him that .

sections.label: Section 542
sections.translation: I must have sprung from mother earth .

sections.label: Section 542b
sections.translation: The ground brings forth no children .

sections.label: Section 543
sections.translation: How can I be thine ?

sections.label: Section 543b
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ , ἀναφέρω δʼ ἐς τὸν θεόν .
sections.translation: I know not ; I refer it to the god .

sections.label: Section 544
sections.translation: Come , let us try another theme .

sections.label: Section 544b
sections.translation: Better hold to this , my son .

sections.label: Section 545
sections.translation: Didst thou e ’ er indulge in illicit amours ?

sections.label: Section 545b
sections.translation: Yes , in the folly of youth .

sections.label: Section 546
sections.translation: Ere thou didst win Erechtheus ’ daughter ?

sections.label: Section 546b
sections.translation: Never since .

sections.label: Section 547
sections.translation: Could it be , then , thou didst beget me ?

sections.label: Section 547b
sections.translation: The time coincides therewith .

sections.label: Section 548
sections.translation: In that case , how came I hither ?

sections.label: Section 548b
sections.translation: That puzzles me .

sections.label: Section 549
sections.translation: After that long journey too ?

sections.label: Section 549b
sections.translation: That , too , perplexes me .

sections.label: Section 550
sections.translation: Didst thou in days gone by come to the Pythian rock ?

sections.label: Section 550b
sections.translation: Yes , to join in the mystic rites of Bacchus .

sections.label: Section 551
sections.translation: Didst thou lodge with one of the public hosts ?

sections.label: Section 551b
sections.translation: With one who at Delphi —

sections.label: Section 552
sections.translation: Initiated thee ? or what is it thou sayest ?

sections.label: Section 552b
sections.translation: Among the frantic votaries of Bacchus .

sections.label: Section 553
sections.translation: Wert thou sober , or in thy cups ?

sections.label: Section 553b
sections.translation: I had indulged in the pleasures of the wine-cup .

sections.label: Section 554
sections.translation: That is just the history of my birth .

sections.label: Section 554b
sections.translation: Fate hath discovered thee , my son .

sections.label: Section 555
sections.translation: How came I to the temple ?

sections.label: Section 555b
sections.translation: Maybe the maid exposed thee .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.translation: I have escaped the shame of slavish birth .

sections.label: Section 556b
sections.translation: Acknowledge then thy father , my son .

sections.label: Section 557
sections.original: τῷ θεῷ γοῦν οὐκ ἀπιστεῖν εἰκός .
sections.translation: It is not right that I should mistrust the god .

sections.label: Section 557b
sections.translation: Thou art right there .
